ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) ProxyLogon — w3wp.exe / UMWorkerProcess.exe spawning cmd / PowerShell / recon LOLBins on Exchange
ucs.description: Detects the post-exploitation chain Microsoft attributed to HAFNIUM after weaponising the ProxyLogon SSRF/auth-bypass chain (CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065). Once a China-Chopper-style .aspx web shell is dropped into Exchange OWA/ECP virtual directories, the IIS worker (w3wp.exe) or Unified Messaging worker (UMWorkerProcess.exe) becomes the parent of cmd.exe, PowerShell, net.exe, nltest.exe, procdump.exe, etc. — a relationship that should virtually never occur on a healthy Exchange server.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.process_name) as child values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","UMWorkerProcess.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","whoami.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","tasklist.exe","nltest.exe","arp.exe","ping.exe","mshta.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","csc.exe","makecab.exe","7z.exe","rar.exe") AND Processes.user!="*$" by host Processes.parent_process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// HAFNIUM / Silk Typhoon — Exchange IIS worker spawning recon / shell / archive LOLBins
let susp_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","whoami.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","tasklist.exe","nltest.exe","arp.exe","ping.exe","mshta.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","csc.exe","makecab.exe","7z.exe","rar.exe"]);
let exch_workers   = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","UMWorkerProcess.exe"]);
let ProcSig =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (exch_workers)
    | where FileName in~ (susp_children)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"                       // exclude SYSTEM / machine acct
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
              Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              Child = FileName,
              ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              SHA256;
// Bonus: web-shell drops to known HAFNIUM-targeted Exchange paths
let FileSig =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
    | where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx"
    | where FolderPath has_any (
        @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",
        @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\",
        @"\Exchange Server\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\",
        @"\Exchange Server\V15\ClientAccess\ecp\auth\")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (exch_workers)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName,
              AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              Child = "<webshell-drop>",
              ChildCmd = strcat("FILE:", FolderPath, FileName),
              SHA256;
union ProcSig, FileSig
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// HAFNIUM ProxyLogon — Exchange IIS / UM worker spawning recon-LOLBins (4688)
let susp_children = dynamic(["\\cmd.exe","\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe","\\net.exe","\\net1.exe","\\cscript.exe","\\wscript.exe","\\whoami.exe","\\ipconfig.exe","\\systeminfo.exe","\\procdump.exe","\\procdump64.exe","\\tasklist.exe","\\nltest.exe","\\arp.exe","\\mshta.exe","\\certutil.exe","\\bitsadmin.exe","\\csc.exe","\\makecab.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688                                       // process create
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
     or ParentProcessName endswith "\\UMWorkerProcess.exe"
| where NewProcessName has_any (susp_children)
| where Account !endswith "$"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType, SubjectLogonId
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) — Exchange Worker Process Spawning Recon LOLBins
id: 7c3d1a52-1d51-4f4d-9c02-9f8c0e2a0001
status: stable
description: Detects HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon post-exploitation pattern where the Exchange IIS worker (w3wp.exe) or Unified Messaging worker (UMWorkerProcess.exe) — usually after a ProxyLogon webshell drop — spawns cmd, PowerShell, recon utilities, archivers or LSASS-dump tools.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/06
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.g0125
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\UMWorkerProcess.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\procdump64.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\arp.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\csc.exe'
      - '\makecab.exe'
      - '\7z.exe'
      - '\rar.exe'
  filter_sys:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and not filter_sys
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate Exchange health-script invocations (rare, usually scoped to PowerShell with Microsoft-signed Exchange snap-ins)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM is the canonical ProxyLogon actor (Microsoft MSTIC, March 2021). Their tradecraft is well-documented: drop ASP.NET webshells (China Chopper, Antsword variants) into Exchange OWA/ECP auth virtual directories, then use the shell to invoke a tight LOLBin set — net.exe, nltest.exe, procdump, 7z/rar, certutil, bitsadmin — under the IIS worker. A healthy Exchange server effectivel

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon LSASS credential theft via Procdump / comsvcs.dll MiniDump on internet-facing servers
ucs.description: Hunts the OS-credential dumping techniques HAFNIUM repeatedly used immediately after web-shell access on Exchange / IIS servers — Sysinternals Procdump (`-ma lsass.exe`), `rundll32 comsvcs.dll, MiniDump`, and PowerShell-based MiniDumpWriteDump invocations — narrowed to internet-facing hosts to minimise admin-tool false positives.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where ((Processes.process_name IN ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") AND Processes.process="*lsass*" AND (Processes.process="*-ma*" OR Processes.process="*-mp*")) OR (Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" AND Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*" AND Processes.process="*MiniDump*") OR (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") AND Processes.process="*MiniDumpWriteDump*" AND Processes.process="*lsass*")) AND Processes.user!="*$" by host Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// HAFNIUM / Silk Typhoon — LSASS dump on internet-facing / Exchange hosts
let ExposedHosts =
    DeviceInfo
    | where Timestamp > ago(1d)
    | where IsInternetFacing == true
          or LoggedOnUsers has "Exchange"        // best-effort tag
    | summarize by DeviceId;
let LSASSDump =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where (FileName in~ ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe")
             and ProcessCommandLine has "lsass"
             and ProcessCommandLine has_any (" -ma"," -mp"))
         or (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
             and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll"
             and ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDump")
         or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
             and ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDumpWriteDump"
             and tolower(ProcessCommandLine) has "lsass")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession;
// Side-channel: lsass*.dmp file artefact dropped to disk
let LSASSDmpFile =
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
    | where (FileName matches regex @"(?i)^lsass.*\.dmp$")
         or (FileName endswith ".dmp"
             and FolderPath has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\",
                                     @"\inetpub\", @"\Public\",
                                     @"\Windows\Temp\"))
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              FileName = strcat("FILE:", FolderPath, FileName),
              ProcessCommandLine = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              InitiatingProcessFileName,
              InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession = false;
union LSASSDump, LSASSDmpFile
| join kind=inner ExposedHosts on DeviceId         // narrow to exposed surface
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// HAFNIUM LSASS dumping — Procdump / comsvcs MiniDump / PS MiniDumpWriteDump
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where (NewProcessName has_any ("\\procdump.exe","\\procdump64.exe")
         and CommandLine has "lsass"
         and (CommandLine has " -ma" or CommandLine has " -mp"))
     or (NewProcessName endswith "\\rundll32.exe"
         and CommandLine has "comsvcs.dll"
         and CommandLine has "MiniDump")
     or (NewProcessName has_any ("\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe")
         and CommandLine has "MiniDumpWriteDump"
         and tolower(CommandLine) has "lsass")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) — LSASS Dump via Procdump / comsvcs.dll / PowerShell
id: 8a4b22e3-3f6a-4cd1-9101-72b0d6a90002
status: stable
description: Detects HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon credential-theft tradecraft against LSASS — Sysinternals Procdump (-ma lsass), rundll32 comsvcs.dll MiniDump invocation, and PowerShell MiniDumpWriteDump variants — patterns Microsoft observed during ProxyLogon-era intrusions.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/06
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1218.011
  - attack.g0125
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  procdump:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\procdump64.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'lsass'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -ma'
      - ' -mp'
  comsvcs:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'comsvcs.dll'
      - 'MiniDump'
  ps_minidump:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'MiniDumpWriteDump'
      - 'lsass'
  filter_sys:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: (procdump or comsvcs or ps_minidump) and not filter_sys
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate IR / forensic acquisition activity by approved tooling — allowlist by host or signing identity
level: high
ucs.rationale: Microsoft and Volexity both documented HAFNIUM (G0125 / Silk Typhoon) using exactly this trio of LSASS-dump techniques on freshly-webshelled Exchange hosts — Procdump with `-ma lsass.exe`, the `comsvcs.dll, MiniDump <pid> <out> full` rundll32 trick, and inline PowerShell calling MiniDumpWriteDump. Restricting the Defender variant to internet-facing devices via DeviceInfo cuts the FP load that pure
